hero_subtitle: Traces, logs, and metrics on OpenTelemetry. Query billions of rows in milliseconds — or let your AI agent do it.
trustbar_text: Works with every language OpenTelemetry supports
product_heading: One platform for all your observability data
product_subtitle: From high-level service health to individual span attributes — everything in one place.
features_tracing_desc: Visualize request flows across services with flamegraph and waterfall views. Drill into any span to see attributes, events, and timing.
features_logs_desc: Search and filter logs with full-text search. Correlated with traces for seamless debugging across your stack.
features_metrics_desc: Track request rates, error rates, and latency percentiles. Build custom charts from any metric your services emit.
features_service_heading: All your services at a glance
features_service_desc: See all your services at a glance with latency percentiles, throughput, error rates, and environment badges.
features_errors_heading: Group, track, and fix errors systematically
features_errors_desc: Errors are automatically grouped by type across all your services. See trends over time, affected services, and drill into sample traces to find the root cause.
usecases_ecommerce_heading: Checkout errors spike during a flash sale
usecases_ecommerce_desc: Your flash sale goes live and payment failures start climbing. You need to find the failing service, the exact error, and the upstream cause — fast.
usecases_ecommerce_result: Root cause in 90 seconds: Stripe timeout, correlated with retry-exhaustion logs.
usecases_microservices_heading: API latency doubles without any deployment
usecases_microservices_desc: No one deployed anything, but P95 latency just doubled. The service map shows which dependency is the bottleneck and how the cascade propagates.
usecases_microservices_result: Service map reveals connection pool exhaustion cascading from user-db.
usecases_ai_desc: An alert fires at 3 AM. Instead of paging your on-call, the MCP agent connects to Maple, investigates the issue, and posts a diagnosis to Slack.
usecases_ai_result: MCP agent auto-diagnoses and posts to Slack before anyone wakes up.
usecases_diagnosis_text: Stripe API timeout causing payment failures. Connection pool at capacity. Recommend circuit breaker activation.
otel_heading: The future of observability is open
otel_vendor_desc: Your instrumentation works with any OTel-compatible backend. Switch providers without changing a line of application code.
otel_future_desc: Backed by every major cloud provider and the CNCF. OpenTelemetry is the second most active CNCF project after Kubernetes.
otel_signals_desc: Traces, logs, and metrics from one SDK. No need to cobble together multiple vendor agents and libraries.
otel_community_desc: Thousands of contributors. Auto-instrumentation for every framework. A thriving ecosystem you can trust.
tech_columnar_desc: Queries over billions of rows in milliseconds. Powered by a columnar engine designed for observability workloads.
tech_correlated_desc: Jump from a trace to its logs, from a log to its metric. Every signal is connected by trace and span IDs.
tech_realtime_desc: Data is queryable within seconds of ingestion. No waiting for batch jobs or delayed indexes.
tech_connectors_desc: Pull data from Cloudflare Logpush and Prometheus scrape targets alongside your OpenTelemetry signals.
ai_heading: Let AI agents fix your issues
ai_desc: Maple has a built-in MCP server and AI agent that can query your traces, logs, errors, and metrics directly. Connect any MCP-compatible client and let agents auto-diagnose production issues.
ai_alert_threshold: Error rate for api-gateway exceeded 5% threshold
ai_investigating: Investigating the error spike. Let me check system health first.
ai_above_threshold: Error rate is at 8.4%, well above threshold. Let me find the specific errors.
ai_inspecting: 142 ConnectionTimeout errors. Inspecting a trace to find the root cause.
ai_root_cause: Root cause: db.query span is timing out in the users service. The database connection pool is exhausted.
chat_desc: A built-in AI assistant that understands your traces, logs, and metrics. Ask questions in plain language and get answers with direct links to the relevant data.
chat_user_msg: What caused the latency spike on payment-service in the last hour?
chat_response_1: I found a latency spike starting at 14:12 UTC. The P95 jumped from 140ms to 890ms.
chat_response_2: Root cause: 3 slow database queries in the checkout flow averaging 720ms each. The connection pool hit its max limit (20/20).
dashboard_heading: Build dashboards that tell the story
dashboard_desc: Drag-and-drop widgets, pick from pre-built presets, or let AI suggest the right charts for your services. Export and share as JSON.
dashboard_ai_text: Add a P95 latency chart for payment-service
alert_rules_desc: 7 built-in signal types — error rate, P95/P99 latency, Apdex, throughput, custom metrics, and custom queries. With severity levels, incident tracking, and auto-resolution.
alert_route_desc: Push alerts to Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, or any webhook. Your team gets notified where they already work.
how_step1_desc: Add the OpenTelemetry SDK to your app. Auto-instrumentation handles the rest.
how_step2_desc: Point the OTLP exporter at Maple. Traces, logs, and metrics flow automatically.
how_step3_desc: Open the dashboard and start exploring. Everything is queryable, filterable, and fast.
bottomcta_subtitle: Start sending traces, logs, and metrics in under five minutes. No credit card required.
footer_tagline: Open-source observability platform built on OpenTelemetry.
pricing_trial_notice: You won't be charged for {duration} days · Credit card required
pricing_enterprise_desc: Tailored pricing for high-volume teams with dedicated support and custom retention.
faq_ingestion_a: Maple measures ingestion by the volume of data you send — logs, traces, and metrics are each measured in gigabytes. Each plan includes a monthly data allowance per signal type. Check the plans above for exact limits.
faq_limits_q: What happens if I exceed my plan limits?
faq_limits_a: We'll notify you as you approach your limits. On the Starter plan, ingestion pauses once you hit the cap. On the Startup plan, additional usage is billed at transparent per-GB rates with no surprises.
faq_retention_a: Retention varies by plan — the Starter plan includes 14-day retention, Startup includes 30 days, and Enterprise plans offer custom retention periods. All plans include full query access to your retained data.
faq_otel_a: Yes — Maple is built natively on OpenTelemetry. Any application instrumented with OTel SDKs or the OTel Collector can send data directly to Maple with zero code changes. No proprietary agents required.
faq_selfhost_a: Absolutely. Maple is fully open source and can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure. The cloud plan is available for teams that prefer a managed experience.
faq_trial_q: Do you offer a free trial for paid plans?
faq_trial_a: Yes, all plans include a free trial so you can explore all features before committing.
page_home_title: Open Source Observability for Traces, Logs & Metrics — Maple
page_home_desc: Open-source observability platform built on OpenTelemetry. Collect, visualize, and analyze distributed traces, logs, and metrics from your services with AI-powered diagnostics.
page_pricing_desc: Start free with generous data limits for logs, traces, and metrics. Scale with transparent, usage-based pricing. No per-seat fees, no hidden costs.
page_pricing_subtitle: Start free with generous limits. Scale as you grow with usage-based pricing — no per-seat fees, no hidden costs, no vendor lock-in.
page_otel_desc: Maple is built natively on OpenTelemetry. Ingest traces, logs, and metrics via OTLP with full spec compliance — no proprietary agents, no vendor lock-in.
page_otel_heading: Built on OpenTelemetry from the ground up
page_otel_subtitle: Maple is a fully OTel-native observability platform. Every signal — traces, logs, and metrics — flows through the open standard. No proprietary agents, no SDK lock-in.
page_otel_what_is_p1: OpenTelemetry is the CNCF open-source standard for collecting observability data from distributed systems. It provides a single, vendor-neutral set of APIs, SDKs, and tools to instrument your applications and export traces, logs, and metrics.
page_otel_what_is_p2: With support for every major language and framework, OTel has become the industry default for telemetry instrumentation — backed by contributions from hundreds of organizations.
page_otel_no_vendor_desc: Your instrumentation stays the same regardless of which backend you choose. Switch providers without touching application code.
page_otel_one_standard_desc: Traces, logs, and metrics share a unified data model with correlated context. No stitching together separate tools.
page_otel_community_desc: Backed by the CNCF and hundreds of contributors. The spec evolves with real-world needs, not a single vendor's roadmap.
page_otel_language_desc: First-party SDKs for Go, Java, Python, JavaScript, .NET, Rust, and more. Auto-instrumentation available for most frameworks.
page_otel_future_desc: As the industry converges on OTel, your investment in instrumentation carries forward. No more rip-and-replace migrations.
page_otel_ecosystem_desc: Collectors, exporters, processors, and connectors — a full pipeline you can customize to match your infrastructure.
page_otel_spec_otlp_desc: Send telemetry directly via OTLP/gRPC or OTLP/HTTP. No translation layers, no proprietary formats.
page_otel_spec_semantic_desc: Maple understands and indexes OTel semantic conventions — HTTP, database, RPC, messaging — out of the box.
page_otel_spec_signals_desc: First-class support for traces, logs, and metrics. Each signal is stored, queried, and correlated natively.
page_otel_spec_collector_desc: Use the OpenTelemetry Collector to route, filter, and batch telemetry before sending it to Maple.
page_otel_spec_sdk_desc: Works with every official OTel SDK. If it speaks OTLP, Maple can ingest it — no vendor-specific libraries required.
page_otel_spec_context_desc: W3C Trace Context and Baggage propagation supported. Correlate spans, logs, and metrics across service boundaries.
page_otel_ecosystem_heading: Works with your existing OTel setup
page_otel_ecosystem_p1: Already using the OpenTelemetry Collector, auto-instrumentation agents, or OTel SDKs? Point your OTLP exporter at Maple and start seeing data immediately. No code changes, no new agents to deploy.
page_otel_ecosystem_p2: Maple fits into the OTel ecosystem as a backend — receiving, storing, and visualizing the telemetry your existing pipeline produces. Swap in Maple without disrupting your instrumentation.
page_roadmap_desc: See what we're building, what's in progress, and what's shipped. Maple's development roadmap is transparent and updated regularly.
page_roadmap_subtitle: A transparent view of Maple's development. See what's shipped, what's in progress, and where we're headed next.
page_dt_desc: Visualize request flows across services with flamegraph and waterfall views. Drill into any span to see attributes, events, and timing.
page_dt_subtitle: Visualize request flows with flamegraph, waterfall, and flow views. See exactly where time is spent across your distributed system.
page_dt_waterfall_view_desc: See the full timeline of a request with nested spans, timing bars, and service colors.
page_dt_flamegraph_desc: Identify hot paths and time-consuming operations with interactive flamegraph visualization.
page_dt_flow_desc: Understand service-to-service communication with an interactive dependency flow diagram.
page_dt_attributes_desc: Inspect HTTP headers, database queries, error messages, and custom attributes on any span.
page_dt_correlation_desc: Jump from any span directly to its correlated logs. Every signal connected by trace ID.
page_dt_filtering_desc: Filter traces by service, duration, status, HTTP method, and custom attributes.
page_dd_title: Datadog Alternative (Open Source) — Maple vs Datadog
page_dd_desc: Compare Maple and Datadog for observability. Maple offers transparent usage-based pricing, native OpenTelemetry support, and AI-powered diagnostics — fully open source.
page_dd_subtitle: Datadog is powerful but comes with complex per-host pricing and proprietary agents. Maple delivers the same distributed tracing, logs, and metrics with transparent usage-based pricing, native OpenTelemetry support, and zero vendor lock-in.
page_dd_transparent_desc: Simple usage-based pricing with no per-host fees, no hidden costs, and no surprise bills at the end of the month.
page_dd_otel_desc: Built from the ground up on OpenTelemetry standards. No proprietary agents to install or maintain — use the instrumentation you already have.
page_dd_ai_desc: Diagnose issues faster with AI-powered root cause analysis and MCP tool integration for automated observability workflows.
page_dd_oss_desc: Inspect every line of code, contribute features, and trust that your observability platform has no black boxes.
page_dd_nolock_desc: Your data stays in open formats. Switch providers or self-host at any time — your instrumentation never needs to change.
page_dd_selfhost_desc: Run Maple on your own infrastructure for complete data sovereignty, compliance, and cost control.
page_ecommerce_desc: Debug checkout failures, payment timeouts, and inventory issues in real-time with distributed tracing and log correlation.
page_ecommerce_subtitle: When your flash sale hits and payments start failing, Maple shows you exactly what's happening across every service.
page_ecommerce_step1_desc: Your monitoring detects an error rate spike on the payment service. The alert includes the threshold breach and affected service, giving you immediate context.
page_ecommerce_step2_desc: Click into a failing trace to see the full request path. The payment span is immediately visible in red, showing a 5000ms timeout on the Stripe API call.
page_ecommerce_step3_desc: Jump to correlated logs for the failing span. The Stripe API timeout and retry exhaustion are immediately visible, confirming the root cause without switching tools.
page_ecommerce_cap_tracing_desc: Follow checkout requests from cart to payment to confirmation. See every service hop with timing.
page_ecommerce_cap_logs_desc: Every failed payment links to its retry logs and timeout errors. One click from trace to logs.
page_ecommerce_cap_errors_desc: Group payment errors by type, see trends, fix systematically. No more searching through log files.
page_nextjs_desc: Add distributed tracing, logging, and metrics to your Next.js app in minutes. Built on OpenTelemetry with zero vendor lock-in.
page_nextjs_subtitle: Add distributed tracing to your Next.js application in minutes. Capture server components, API routes, and middleware spans automatically with Vercel's built-in instrumentation hook.
